author: Grub Basket
description: Excel-grade editable grid over your notes' frontmatter: multi-cell selection, fill down/right, find & replace in selection, keyboard-first editing, heading-content columns. - This plugin has not been manually reviewed by Obsidian staff.

manifest.author: GB
manifest.description: Excel-grade editable grid over your notes' frontmatter: multi-cell selection, fill down/right, find & replace in selection, keyboard-first editing, heading-content columns.
manifest.name: GridSense

name: GridSense

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## GridSense 0.5.0

- Empty draft rows at the top and bottom of every grid. Drafts are UI-only: no note is created until the row has a name and you press Enter — then the note is created, any property values you typed flush into it, and a fresh draft appears. Abandoning or clearing a draft never touches your files, and clearing cells in a real row never deletes the note.
- Excel-style row stability: once displayed, row order is frozen — editing cells (even the sorted column) and newly created notes never reshuffle the grid. Changing sort, filter, or pressing ↺ re-derives the order. Notes committed from a draft stay pinned to the end of the grid where you made them.
- Fixed: a background recompile could drop its follow-up refresh, leaving the grid on stale rows.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## GridSense 0.4.3

- New setting: default row limit — applied to every grid that has no per-folder limit (0 = unlimited, the default). Per-folder limits in ▦ columns & views override it; clearing the folder field falls back to the plugin default. The row counter always shows when a limit is trimming.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## GridSense 0.4.2

- Removed the silent default row cap (was 2,000): a fresh grid now always shows every note in scope, so you never mistake a truncated grid for the whole folder. Virtualized rendering keeps big folders fast.
- Prominent row counter in the toolbar: "1,234 rows", or "60 of 1,234 rows" (highlighted) whenever a filter or row limit is trimming the view — with a tooltip saying which.
- Grids over 10,000 rows show a warning button that opens the columns & views modal to hide columns or set a row limit.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## GridSense 0.4.1

- Inline properties takeover is now OFF by default while in beta (Settings toggle).
- Property rows have a type button on the left: change property type (Text/List/Number/Checkbox/Date/Date & time), zoom the value, or delete — reserved properties (tags, aliases, cssclasses) are recognized and locked to their list types.
- Values parse by property type: tags strip #, lists split on commas or newlines, numbers and checkboxes coerce properly.
- List values render as chips with a visible x per item (removal is undoable).
- Left/right arrows switch between key and value (same as Tab).
- Fixed: double-clicking a value no longer kicks you out of edit mode; Tab after editing stays in the properties strip instead of jumping to the note body.
- New: cell zoom — a large editor for one value, in the property menu and the grid's right-click menu.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## GridSense 0.4.0

- Inline frontmatter takeover: Obsidian's native properties panel is replaced by GridSense's keyboard-friendly editor in Live Preview and Reading mode (toggle in settings; on by default). Arrow keys navigate, Tab switches key/value, Enter edits, Cmd/Ctrl+Enter adds, Cmd/Ctrl+Backspace deletes, Cmd/Ctrl+Z undoes.
- The properties modal and inline editor now share one component.
- README: install from the community plugin store.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## GridSense 0.3.2

- 0.3.2: view keymap Scope for ⌘D/⌘R/⌘F/⌘Z/⇧⌘Z/⌘Y — beats Obsidian's default hotkeys (⌘D was swallowed by editor:delete-paragraph)

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## GridSense 0.3.1

Every change since 0.3.0:

- 0.3.1: unified undo/redo (frontmatter edits + column hide/show/remove), redo (⇧⌘Z/⌘Y) + toolbar button, phantom-drag fix (e.buttons check), header-click focuses grid so ⌘D works, explicit fill guards both directions

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## GridSense 0.3.0

Every change since 0.2.0:

- 0.3.0: column drag-reorder, display-name rename, new-note footer row, summary footer (sum/avg/filled), view-apply recompiles heading columns

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## GridSense 0.2.0

Every change since 0.1.6:

- README: installation + license sections (publishing prep)
- 0.2.0: virtualized rendering, snapshot fast-paint, sort, filter, content-based column widths + resize, word wrap, row limit, named views, XLOOKUP/XMATCH/heading-mapping formula columns, heading-name display setting

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## GridSense 0.1.6 — initial public release

Everything so far:

- GridSense 0.1.0: folder-scoped editable frontmatter grid (selection, fill, find/replace, heading columns)
- 0.1.0: note property editor modal, optimistic batch writes, README
- 0.1.1: F&R scope dropdown (selection/all/column), columns show-hide modal, heading-link cells, cell context menu
- 0.1.2: searchable find&replace scope picker (suggest, hidden columns targetable, keyed-cell refactor)
- 0.1.3: column-header context menu (hide column, manage columns)
- 0.1.4: hide-column × button on property headers
- 0.1.5: immortal per-grid edit log (JSONL, includes undos) + history modal
- 0.1.6: green pending-updates badge mid-edit + flush held-back refresh on editor close
- Add MIT license (publishing prep)
- README: installation + license sections (publishing prep)
- versions.json for store publication
